text: World Cup 2026: England beat Croatia 1-0 in Group L opener in Dallas. Published: 2026-06-17. Topics: FIFA World Cup 2026, England, Croatia, Football. England secured a narrow but vital 1-0 victory over Croatia in their 2026 FIFA World Cup Group L opener at AT&T Stadium in Dallas. Jude Bellingham scored the only goal of the match with a precise low finish in the 34th minute, converting a cutback from Bukayo Saka. England's defence, marshalled by captain Harry Maguire and Lewis Dunk, held firm despite Croatia's second-half pressure. Manager Gareth Southgate praised the team's discipline, saying the result was exactly what they needed to start the tournament. Croatia, who reached the 2018 final, struggled to create clear chances, with their best opportunity falling to Andrej Kramaric, whose header was well saved by Jordan Pickford. England's next group game is against Ghana in Houston on 21 June.
title: World Cup 2026: England beat Croatia 1-0 in Group L opener in Dallas

text: World Cup 2026: Scotland suffer shock 3-1 defeat to Morocco in Group C. Published: 2026-06-15. Topics: FIFA World Cup 2026, Scotland, Morocco, Football. Scotland's dream debut at their first World Cup since 1998 turned into a nightmare as Morocco ran out comfortable 3-1 winners in their Group C clash in Los Angeles. Achraf Hakimi opened the scoring with a stunning long-range effort before Youssef En-Nesyri added two second-half goals. Scott McTominay pulled one back for Scotland with a powerful header, but it was not enough. Scotland manager Steve Clarke admitted his side were outclassed in the second half, as Morocco's physicality and pace proved too much. Scotland's campaign continues on 19 June when they face five-time champions Brazil in Miami, a match widely seen as their most difficult group stage fixture.
title: World Cup 2026: Scotland suffer shock 3-1 defeat to Morocco in Group C

text: World Cup 2026: Argentina's Messi scores twice as holders beat Australia 4-1. Published: 2026-06-16. Topics: FIFA World Cup 2026, Argentina, Lionel Messi, Football. Defending champions Argentina produced a masterclass as they thrashed Australia 4-1 in their World Cup opener in New York. Lionel Messi, 38, showed no signs of slowing down with two sublime goals — a trademark free-kick and a cool finish from close range — to open his 2026 World Cup account. Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez added the others, while Australia's consolation came from a Mathew Leckie penalty. Despite his age, Messi was involved in every dangerous attacking move, producing 12 key passes over the 90 minutes. Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni confirmed the team are targeting a historic back-to-back World Cup title.
title: World Cup 2026: Argentina's Messi scores twice as holders beat Australia 4-1

text: World Cup 2026: France and Brazil draw 1-1 in blockbuster Group A clash. Published: 2026-06-15. Topics: FIFA World Cup 2026, France, Brazil, Football. Two of the tournament favourites cancelled each other out in a tense 1-1 draw in Miami's Hard Rock Stadium. Kylian Mbappé gave France the lead with a clinical counter-attack goal midway through the first half, only for Vinícius Júnior to equalise with a powerful left-foot strike after the break. Both sides had chances to win — Mbappé hit the post late on and Brazil's Raphinha had a goal disallowed for offside. France manager Didier Deschamps described the result as acceptable given the calibre of opposition. Brazil coach Dorival Júnior insisted the team will improve as the tournament progresses.
title: World Cup 2026: France and Brazil draw 1-1 in blockbuster Group A clash

text: Cricket: England beat New Zealand by seven wickets in first Test at Lord's. Published: 2026-06-08. Topics: Test Cricket, England, New Zealand, Ben Stokes. England cruised to a seven-wicket victory over New Zealand in the first Test of the three-match series at Lord's. Set a target of 186, England openers Zak Crawley and Ben Duckett put on a 143-run partnership to set the tone for a comfortable chase. Ben Stokes, returning from a knee injury, bowled brilliantly in New Zealand's second innings, taking four wickets for 37 runs. New Zealand captain Tom Latham scored a gutsy 94 in the first innings but could not inspire his side to a competitive total. England's victory puts them on top of the ICC World Test Championship standings. The second Test is scheduled for The Oval from 17 June.
title: Cricket: England beat New Zealand by seven wickets in first Test at Lord's

text: Cricket: England women complete T20 World Cup squad ahead of home tournament. Published: 2026-06-10. Topics: Women's T20 World Cup 2026, England Women's Cricket, Heather Knight. England Women's captain Heather Knight has confirmed her 15-player squad for the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, which begins at Headingley on 12 June. Notable inclusions are teenage sensation Freya Kemp, who impressed in the recent series against South Africa, and returning all-rounder Nat Sciver-Brunt following her recovery from a hamstring injury. England are placed in Group B alongside Australia, India, Pakistan and the West Indies — a group commentators have already labelled a 'group of death'. Knight acknowledged the challenging draw but expressed confidence in her squad's depth and home advantage. England's opening match is against Pakistan at Headingley on 14 June.
title: Cricket: England women complete T20 World Cup squad ahead of home tournament

text: Tennis: Carlos Alcaraz beats Novak Djokovic in five-set Queen's Club final. Published: 2026-06-21. Topics: Queen's Club Championships, Carlos Alcaraz, Novak Djokovic, Tennis. Carlos Alcaraz retained his Queen's Club title with a gruelling 6-4 3-6 6-3 4-6 7-5 victory over Novak Djokovic in a match lasting three hours and 47 minutes. The 23-year-old Spaniard came from a set down twice to triumph in a contest of the highest quality on the Wimbledon warm-up grass. Djokovic, 39, pushed the world number one to the limit with his trademark defensive baseline game and mental resilience. Alcaraz saved two match points in the final game before converting his third championship point with an unstoppable forehand winner. Both players will now head into Wimbledon as the leading contenders for the title, which begins on 29 June.
title: Tennis: Carlos Alcaraz beats Novak Djokovic in five-set Queen's Club final

text: Tennis: Iga Swiatek withdraws from Eastbourne with wrist injury ahead of Wimbledon. Published: 2026-06-20. Topics: Eastbourne International, Iga Swiatek, Wimbledon, Tennis. World number one Iga Swiatek has withdrawn from the Eastbourne International with a wrist injury, raising doubts about her fitness ahead of Wimbledon. The Polish player, who won the French Open for a record sixth time last month, was due to play her first-round match on Tuesday. Swiatek's management confirmed she is receiving treatment and will be assessed over the coming days before deciding whether to compete at the All England Club. The 25-year-old has never won Wimbledon and has often struggled on grass. Her potential absence would significantly boost the chances of rivals Aryna Sabalenka and Elena Rybakina.
title: Tennis: Iga Swiatek withdraws from Eastbourne with wrist injury ahead of Wimbledon

text: Formula 1: Max Verstappen wins Canadian Grand Prix to extend championship lead. Published: 2026-06-14. Topics: Formula 1, Canadian Grand Prix, Max Verstappen, Red Bull Racing. Max Verstappen claimed his fourth successive Canadian Grand Prix victory at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal, further extending his lead in the Formula 1 World Championship. Starting from pole position, the Red Bull driver led from start to finish, producing a faultless performance in warm, humid conditions. Lewis Hamilton, in his first season with Ferrari, finished second after a strategically astute two-stop race, while Lando Norris completed the podium for McLaren. Verstappen's victory gives him a 47-point lead over Hamilton in the standings with 11 races remaining. The next race is the British Grand Prix at Silverstone on 5 July.
title: Formula 1: Max Verstappen wins Canadian Grand Prix to extend championship lead

text: Formula 1: Ferrari confirm Charles Leclerc will miss British Grand Prix with back injury. Published: 2026-06-18. Topics: Formula 1, Charles Leclerc, Ferrari, British Grand Prix. Ferrari have confirmed that Charles Leclerc will sit out the British Grand Prix at Silverstone on 5 July after suffering a back injury during a training accident. The Monégasque driver will be replaced by reserve driver Antonio Giovinazzi, who last raced in Formula 1 in 2021. Leclerc is expected to be fit for the Hungarian Grand Prix the following weekend. Ferrari team principal Frédéric Vasseur expressed his disappointment but confirmed the team's focus is on Leclerc's recovery. The news is a blow to Ferrari's constructors' championship hopes as they trail Red Bull by 38 points.
title: Formula 1: Ferrari confirm Charles Leclerc will miss British Grand Prix with back injury

text: Rugby Union: British and Irish Lions name 37-man squad for Australia tour. Published: 2026-06-09. Topics: British and Irish Lions, Rugby Union, Australia Tour 2026. Head coach Warren Gatland has named a 37-man British and Irish Lions squad for the tour of Australia, which begins on 27 June. The squad contains 14 England players, 11 from Ireland, seven from Wales and five from Scotland. Notable inclusions are uncapped England flanker Tom Willis, who has been in outstanding form for Saracens, and Ireland fly-half Jack Crowley, who is expected to challenge Finn Russell for the Test starting spot. Experienced Wales lock Adam Beard and Scotland captain Finn Russell are also named. The Lions will play seven matches in total, including three Tests against the Wallabies, who are currently ranked fifth in the world rankings.
title: Rugby Union: British and Irish Lions name 37-man squad for Australia tour

text: Rugby Union: Ireland beat South Africa 28-19 in Lions tour warm-up at Aviva Stadium. Published: 2026-06-13. Topics: Rugby Union, Ireland, South Africa, British and Irish Lions warm-up. Ireland produced an impressive display to beat South Africa 28-19 in a Lions tour warm-up match at the Aviva Stadium in Dublin, giving several Lions hopefuls a chance to impress coach Warren Gatland. Jack Crowley ran the game superbly, finishing with 18 points from the tee and orchestrating Ireland's flowing attack. South Africa, resting several key players ahead of their Rugby Championship commitments, were competitive but could not match Ireland's intensity. Flanker Caelan Doris was outstanding and is now considered a near-certainty to start the first Lions Test. The result increases the excitement around the Lions tour, which departs for Australia in two weeks.
title: Rugby Union: Ireland beat South Africa 28-19 in Lions tour warm-up at Aviva Stadium

text: Golf: Rory McIlroy wins US Open for second time at Pinehurst No. 2. Published: 2026-06-22. Topics: US Open Golf, Rory McIlroy, Pinehurst, Major Championship. Rory McIlroy finally added a second US Open title to his collection, holding his nerve to win by two strokes at Pinehurst No. 2 in North Carolina. The 37-year-old Northern Irishman fired a final-round 68 to finish on 12 under par, seeing off the challenge of Scottie Scheffler, who finished joint second alongside Ludvig Åberg. McIlroy, who also won the US Open in 2011, has now won six major championships in total. Visibly emotional as he walked up the 18th fairway, he dedicated the win to his family and mentioned the mental health challenges he has overcome in recent years. The victory cements McIlroy's status as one of the greatest golfers of his generation.
title: Golf: Rory McIlroy wins US Open for second time at Pinehurst No. 2

text: Golf: Scottish Open field revealed with 12 world top-10 players confirmed. Published: 2026-06-11. Topics: Scottish Open Golf, Genesis Scottish Open, Renaissance Club. Organisers of the Genesis Scottish Open have confirmed a world-class field for this year's event at Renaissance Club in North Berwick from 8-11 July. Twelve of the world's top ten players have committed to compete, including world number one Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy and Jon Rahm. The tournament serves as the final Rolex Series event before The Open Championship at Royal Portrush the following week. Prize money has been increased to $12 million for 2026, making it the richest event on the European Tour. Scottish fans will be hoping for home success from Robert MacIntyre, who won the event in 2024.
title: Golf: Scottish Open field revealed with 12 world top-10 players confirmed

text: Athletics: Keely Hodgkinson breaks 800m world record in Oslo Diamond League. Published: 2026-06-05. Topics: Athletics, Diamond League Oslo, Keely Hodgkinson, 800m World Record. Olympic champion Keely Hodgkinson shattered the 800m world record at the Bislett Games Diamond League meeting in Oslo, clocking 1:53.28 to break the previous mark of 1:53.28 set by Jarmila Kratochvílová in 1983. The 24-year-old from Wigan ran an almost perfect race, taking the lead at the bell and pulling clear in the home straight to finish five metres clear of the field. A stunned Bislett Stadium erupted as the time appeared on the scoreboard. Hodgkinson, who won Olympic gold at Paris 2024, said she had been targeting the record all season and was overwhelmed with emotion. The performance makes her the overwhelming favourite for Commonwealth Games gold in Glasgow in July.
title: Athletics: Keely Hodgkinson breaks 800m world record in Oslo Diamond League

text: Athletics: Mo Farah announces return to competitive running at age 43. Published: 2026-06-07. Topics: Athletics, Mo Farah, Comeback, Road Running. Four-time Olympic champion Mo Farah has announced a surprise return to competitive running, confirming he will race the Great North Run in Newcastle on 13 September. The 43-year-old, who retired from track athletics in 2023, revealed he has been training consistently over the past year and wants to prove he can still compete at the highest level in road racing. Farah's management confirmed he has no plans to return to track events but is targeting a sub-61-minute performance at the half marathon. Athletics fans and fellow competitors have reacted with a mixture of excitement and scepticism, with many questioning whether the challenge is realistic at his age.
title: Athletics: Mo Farah announces return to competitive running at age 43

text: Cycling: Tadej Pogačar wins stage 12 of Tour de France to extend overall lead. Published: 2026-06-06. Topics: Tour de France, Cycling, Tadej Pogačar, Jonas Vingegaard. Wait — Tour de France doesn't begin until 27 June 2026. This is a preview: Tadej Pogačar has been installed as the 8/11 favourite to claim a hat-trick of Tour de France titles, having won the Giro d'Italia and Vuelta a España in 2025. His main rival is expected to be defending champion Jonas Vingegaard, who has been in strong form in recent preparation races. UAE Team Emirates director Mauro Gianetti confirmed that the Tour is Pogačar's primary goal for the summer, with all other race selections built around peaking at the Grande Boucle. Pogačar said in a pre-Tour press conference that he feels stronger than ever and is hungry to add to his Grand Tour tally.
title: Cycling: Pogačar installed as Tour de France favourite ahead of 27 June start

text: Boxing: Anthony Joshua stops Daniel Dubois in round seven to reclaim British heavyweight crown. Published: 2026-06-20. Topics: Boxing, Anthony Joshua, Daniel Dubois, Heavyweight Championship. Anthony Joshua reclaimed the British heavyweight title with a seventh-round stoppage of Daniel Dubois at Wembley Stadium in front of 80,000 fans. Joshua, 36, dropped Dubois twice in the fifth round before the referee halted proceedings midway through the seventh after Dubois took three unanswered left hooks to the head. Joshua controlled the fight from the outset with his jab, switching to powerful combinations in the later rounds as Dubois tired. An emotional Joshua told the crowd he wants a unification fight against Tyson Fury or Oleksandr Usyk before the end of 2026. The win improves Joshua's professional record to 27-4.
title: Boxing: Anthony Joshua stops Daniel Dubois in round seven to reclaim British heavyweight crown

text: Swimming: Adam Peaty announces retirement from competitive swimming at 31. Published: 2026-06-12. Topics: Swimming, Adam Peaty, Retirement, Breaststroke. Treble Olympic breaststroke champion Adam Peaty has announced his retirement from competitive swimming at the age of 31, ending a career that saw him dominate the 100m breaststroke for over a decade. Peaty, who has held the world record since 2019, said the decision came after careful reflection and that he wants to focus on his family and emerging business interests. He leaves the sport as one of Britain's greatest ever Olympians, having won three Olympic golds, two World Championship titles and set multiple world records. Peaty thanked British Swimming and his coaches for their unwavering support throughout his career. Swimming fans have paid tribute to a genuine legend of the sport.
title: Swimming: Adam Peaty announces retirement from competitive swimming at 31

text: Commonwealth Games: Glasgow 2026 torch relay begins journey across UK. Published: 2026-06-19. Topics: Commonwealth Games 2026, Glasgow, Torch Relay, Multi-Sport Event. The Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games Queen's Baton Relay has arrived in the United Kingdom, beginning a six-week journey across all four home nations before the opening ceremony on 23 July. The baton was handed over in Edinburgh at a ceremony attended by Scotland's First Minister and over five thousand spectators. Over the next six weeks the relay will travel through more than 300 communities in Scotland, England, Wales and Northern Ireland, carried by athletes, community champions and young people. Glasgow previously hosted the 2014 Commonwealth Games and organisers have promised a bigger and better event this time, with 72 nations competing across 19 sports.
title: Commonwealth Games: Glasgow 2026 torch relay begins journey across UK
